meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: A Student's Guide to Building SaaS
content.seo_description: Master AI-driven debugging to break through coding plateaus. An intermediate checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to level up your development skills.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at cryptic stack traces. By integrating AI-assisted debugging, you transform errors into learning opportunities. Use Antigravity to map your architecture and Claude Code to iterate on logic. This methodology accelerates your development cycle, allowing you to move from basic scripts to complex, production-ready SaaS applications with confidence.
content.intro:
You’ve passed the 'Hello World' phase, but now you’re hitting the wall. Your terminal is a graveyard of unhandled promise rejections and silent failures. You’re not just stuck; you’re experiencing the threshold of moving from a student who copies tutorials to an engineer who builds systems.

Relief isn't found in another YouTube video. It’s found in changing your relationship with the debugger. By leveraging AI as your pair programmer, you stop fighting the code and start architecting solutions. This guide is your blueprint to mastering the AI-driven debugging workflow that separates junior coders from high-impact builders.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure State
steps.description: Before throwing code at an AI, define the exact error boundary. Use your tools to log the input/output of the failing function.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this module's execution flow. Identify the specific variable state that deviates from expected behavior during runtime.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'find the function causing the TypeError in src/api/handler.ts'

steps.title: Architectural Context Injection
steps.description: AI models need the 'Big Picture.' Provide the schema and dependencies to ensure the fix doesn't break adjacent modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Map the dependencies for this service. Explain how this function interacts with the database schema and external API wrappers.

steps.title: Generate Defensive Patches
steps.description: Use Claude Code to draft potential fixes. Prioritize solutions that include error handling and type safety.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose three refactoring options for this logic to handle null pointers and asynchronous race conditions effectively.

steps.title: Verify Through Synthetic Testing
steps.description: Don't just trust the fix. Generate a test case that specifically triggers the previous error to confirm resolution.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a unit test suite that covers the edge cases identified during our debugging session.

steps.title: Documentation of the 'Why'
steps.description: Document the bug fix within the codebase so you don't repeat the mistake in your next project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a concise JSDoc comment explaining the logic fix and why the original implementation was flawed.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit 'fix: resolve race condition in processor logic'

pro_tips: Always ask the AI to explain 'why' a fix works, not just 'how' to implement it; this is your primary learning mechanism.
Use Antigravity to visualize your data flow before writing code; debugging is 80% understanding the architecture.
Treat your AI tools as junior developers; review their code for security vulnerabilities before merging.
Maintain a 'bug log' file in your repo to track common patterns you encounter, allowing the AI to learn your specific coding style.
Limit context windows by splitting large files into smaller modules; AI performs significantly better on modular codebases.
resources: MDN Web Docs for Error Handling: https://developer.mozilla.org
content.next_steps: Once your bug is resolved, refactor the surrounding code to improve readability and push your changes to your portfolio repository to showcase your problem-solving skills.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student struggling with errors; you are a builder mastering the tools of the future. Stop letting bugs dictate your progress. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
